search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG153 psoriasis assessment distribution nails joints impact
rejection_reasons: Treatment algorithms were outside documentation-only scope.
Medicine dose tables were outside scope.
Image classifiers could imply autonomous diagnosis.
population_applicability: Direct for children, young people, and adults with established or suspected psoriasis, with age-specific interpretation required.
setting_applicability: Direct for primary and specialist psoriasis assessment fields; referral and treatment decisions remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE psoriasis guideline was identified; NICE evidence requires UAE clinician adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE CG153, last updated 2017-09-01, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG153 remains current on the NICE site.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports psoriasis identity, plaques, distribution, scale, nails, symptoms, extent, systemic upset, and life impact.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports joint-symptom screening and clinician-arranged referral only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking about joint symptoms and recording clinician-led specialist assessment or referral only when completed or arranged.

unresolved_source_gaps: Fever, blistering, infection, blood tests, photographs, skin-care advice, trigger review, fixed follow-up, medicine, and treatment fields remain unsupported.
Qualified UAE clinician review is required.
